calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Nov-07-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared via zoom for and with Franza Giffen (petitioner).

Ms. Harrington stated that the two payments were to pay for the home improvements made by Mr. Wolf a general contractor. The unpaid $435.00 filing fee to be paid thru the distirbution of Benjamin Arseguel. The Petition for final distribution etc., was taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Prieto: Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Amy Harrington, Esq. for Franza Giffen (Petitioner/Trustee) who also appeared via Zoom.

The Court stated that it had read the first supplemental account, that it is not offended by the trustee's fees and that it was laudable to make it work, that it understands that Ms. Harrington was simply advising the Court about the trustee's fees before the matter was taken under court supervision, and that it understands that the order will only be for fees after the trust was taken under court supervision. The Court also stated that there was no objection to approve fees to Ms. Harrington or her client and noted that it does not have a current accounting to reduce the bond. Ms. Harrington stated that the taxes for 2022 need to be done, that there will be a closing reserve, and that there will be an accounting through January 1st.

At the conclusion of comments, the First and Supplemental Accounts and Reports of Successor Trustee, Petition for Settlement Thereof, For Decrease in Bond, and for Payment of Fees and Costs to Successor Trustee and her Attorney is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Cho. Matter not reported.

REPORT OF SALE
Off Calendar Jun-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington, via Zoom, for and with Franza Giffen, Successor Trustee.

The court points to the issue of whether the proof of publication filed in this case is sufficient to proceed with the Report of Sale this morning. Ms. Harrington offers testimony in support of proceeding with the sale of the real property this morning. After brief discussion, the sale was confirmed at $1,250,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). Additional bond is not required.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

Petition For Authority To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And To Execute Note And Deed Of Trust Mortgage Security Instrument And Loan Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Advanced From Nov-03-20 To Sep-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And To Execute Note And Deed Of Trust Mortgage Security Instrument And Loan Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-15-298967 Conservatorship of Myriam W. Oliven and
PTR-15-299149 The Myriam W. Oliven Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared by zoom for and with Franza Giffen (Successor Trustee/Conservator) by zoom.
Ernest Der (Court appointed Counsel) by zoom for Myriam Oliven (Conservatee).
Benjamin Arseguel (Son of Conservatee0 appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that this petition has been on calendar several times, that Mr. Arseguel was to file his objections with an alternative plan and that it does not have anything. The Court also stated that it has read everything, that it appreciates the information that Mr. Arseguel has provided, that it is clear that Mr. Arseguel loves his Mother very much and she loves him, that there is no dispute about that and that there is no dispute that Ms. Oliven wants to stay in her home. In addition, the Court stated that Mr. Arseguel provided ideas, but nothing concrete and that what was provided does not comply with the Health, Safety and Welfare Code requirements. Mr. Arseguel expressed his concerns about the way his Mother is being managed, stated that he is ready to take care of his Mother, expressed frustration regarding the parties' involvement with his Mother's affairs, stated that he cannot co-exist with the other Parties and spoke of his Mother's medical condition. Mr. Der stated that Ms. Oliven would like to stay in her house, that she was concerned about the costs and that he believes a reverse mortgage is the way to go. Ms. Harrington stated that there is nothing preventing Mr. Arseguel to petition the Court with a formalized plan and that they need a reverse mortgage to continue with the current plan that is working. The court noted that the conservatorship was started over five years ago, that the only thing before it is the reverse mortgage and that if Mr. Arseguel wants to initiate a guardianship in Texas and move his Mother there, he has every right to do so. The Petitions for Authority to Obtain Reverse Mortgage are under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And To Execute Note And Deed Of Trust Mortgage Security Instrument And Loan Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Continued To Oct-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington, Esq., counsel for and with Franza Griffen, Conservator (appearing separately by video conference); Benjamin Arseguel, interested person, in pro per.

Heard with Conservatorship of Myriam W. Oliven - PCN-15-298967

The Court notes at the outset that a previous continuance was granted to allow Mr. Arseguel time to file written opposition to the Petition but none has been received. Mr. Arseguel reports that various attempts were made to file the same but, due to procedural issues, were rejected by the filing clerks. Ms. Harrington reports that she did receive the opposition but did not respond, noting that the same was never filed.

At Ms. Harrington's invitation, Mr. Griffen reports on the consequences of the delay in acquiring the reverse mortgage in that the conservatorship has an outstanding debt to the care giver agency which fees the agency is deferring for the moment. She also reports on the conservatee's income from other sources.

After further discussion, the Court continues the current matters to October 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Mr. Arseguel is directed to file his objections on or before October 13, 2020 with courtesy copies to the Examiner by that date. Ms. Harrington, since she is in receipt of the objections, albeit unfiled, is directed to file her reply on or before October 13, 2020 with courtesy copies to the Examiner by that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (D204)

Petition For Authority To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage And To Execute Note And Deed Of Trust Mortgage Security Instrument And Loan Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Continued To Oct-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-15-298967 Conservatorship of Myriam W. Oliven and
PTR-15-299149 The Myriam W. Oliven Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared by zoom for and with Franza Giffen (Successor Trustee/Conservator) by zoom.
Ernest Der (Court appointed Counsel) appeared by zoom for Myriam Oliven (Conservatee).
Benjamin Arsequel (Son of Conservatee) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Mr. Arsequel stated that he is objecting to the petition, that a promise was made to his Mother that she would take her last breath at home, that this would only allow her three years and that he would like to represent his Mother's point of view. The Court clarified that Mr. Arsequel does not represent the Conservatee, and that the Conservatee has an Attorney and a Conservator. The Court also informed the parties that it is going to continue the matter for Mr. Arsequel to submit his objections in writing and serve all parties. In addition, the Court suggested that Mr. Arsequel read the 6th amended general order so that he is familiar with the required procedures. Ms. Harrington stated that they had requested advancement of this hearing because Ms. Oliven is out of funds, and that Ms. Giffen has arranged for the Caregivers to continue to work and get paid later. Ms. Giffen stated that the 24-hour Caregivers will not be paid for this month, that the reverse mortgage people stated that as the real estate gains more value, they will look at this again, and that the Conservatee is out of funds. The Petition for Authority to Engage in Counseling and Execute any Documents Necessary to Obtain a Reverse Mortgage, etc. is continued to October 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Arsequel that he needs to get this done in two weeks.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition For Order Advancing Hearing Date And Shortening Time For Notice
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Confirm That Franza Giffen Is The Duly Appointed Successor Trustee Of The Myriam W. Oliven Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Nov-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for Franza Giffen. Court Appointed counsel Ernest Der for Myriam Oliven. Karla Gottschalk, trustee.

Ms. Gottschalk stated that she has been denied access to her client and funds, that if the trust was on for 8/13 someone should have told her, that the 1/29/90 trust may be valid, that the nomination of the 1/23/90 trust is unknown and invalid and that the 8/12 appointment is valid. Ms. Harrington stated that she has read the trust, that Ms. Giffen is the conservator and is currently working with Ms. Oliven. Mr. Der stated that he met with his client and went through the pleadings, that Ms. Oliven did not recall meeting Ms. Gottschalk, that she did not remember seeing the objections, that Ms. Oliven expressed a willingness to work with Ms. Giffen, that Ms. Oliven has been told that they could find someone else, that Ms. Oliven believes that Ms. Payne has ruined her life and that she want to think about another trustee. Ms. Gottschald commented that there is no court reporter, that Mr. Der's comments are hearsay, that the doctors reports were not submitted at the conservatorship hearing, that Ms. Oliven called her regarding insects in her house and was given information to call Mr. Todd for extermination, that Mr. Todd states that Ms. Oliven is capable, that there is undue influence going on and that Ms. Gottschalk takes care of her. The petition to confirm that Franza Giffen is the duly appointed successor trustee of the Myriam W. Oliven revocable trust was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
